summary: Re-linked dolphin/ar/arq.c as matching by making two helper-like functions static inline, removing their external declarations from arq.h, and adjusting the GALE01 split boundary for adjacent dolphin/dsp/dsp_task.c data. The PR is very small and has no body or review comments, but the diff shows a typical decomp relinking cleanup: tighten linkage/header exposure so codegen and object boundaries match, then flip arq.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py.
what_changed: Changed configure.py RuntimeLib ar object list so dolphin/ar/arq.c is built as Object(Matching, "dolphin/ar/arq.c") instead of NonMatching.
Changed src/dolphin/ar/arq.c so __ARQPopTaskQueueHi is declared as static inline instead of externally visible void.
Changed src/dolphin/ar/arq.c so ARQGetChunkSize is declared as static inline instead of externally visible size_t.

Removed ARQGetChunkSize and __ARQPopTaskQueueHi declarations from src/dolphin/ar/arq.h, leaving only externally needed ARQ API/internal hook declarations such as ARQReset, ARQPostRequest, __ARQServiceQueueLo, __ARQCallbackHack, and __ARQInitTempQueue.
Adjusted config/GALE01/splits.txt for dolphin/dsp/dsp_task.c .data end from 0x80400D80 to 0x80400D7B, indicating a relink-induced data boundary correction near neighboring Dolphin objects.
smart_moves: Used static inline on functions that did not need public header exposure, likely matching original linkage/codegen expectations without changing call-site behavior visible in the excerpt.
Kept the public header cleaner by removing declarations for functions made file-local, reducing accidental cross-object linkage pressure.
Made the relink explicit by flipping only arq.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, while leaving dolphin/ar/ar.c nonmatching.

Included a split-boundary adjustment in the same PR, which is important for relinking because object matching can expose small section-size/address mismatches outside the immediately edited source file.
decomp_lessons: When a Dolphin SDK object is close to matching but still nonmatching, check whether helper functions are incorrectly exported through headers; original SDK-local helpers may need static inline/file-local treatment.
Relinking a single source file can require config/GALE01/splits.txt updates for neighboring section boundaries, so verify map/split data after changing Matching status.
Header declarations should reflect actual cross-translation-unit requirements, not every function present in a C file; excessive declarations can force different linkage than the original binary.
A successful relink PR may be only a few line changes: matching status, linkage qualifiers, and split metadata can be enough if the implementation was already correct.
naming_conventions: Dolphin ARQ internal globals/functions use double-underscore prefixes such as __ARQChunkSize, __ARQ_init_flag, __ARQPopTaskQueueHi, __ARQRequestQueueHi, __ARQServiceQueueLo, __ARQCallbackHack, and __ARQInitTempQueue.
Public ARQ APIs use ARQ-prefixed names such as ARQReset, ARQPostRequest, and ARQGetChunkSize.
Types and enums shown in the diff/context use ARQ-prefixed names: ARQRequest, ARQType, ARQPrio, ARQCallback, and ARQ_TYPE_MRAM_TO_ARAM.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Marking __ARQPopTaskQueueHi as static inline suggests the expected binary did not contain or reference it as a normal external symbol from this translation unit.
Marking ARQGetChunkSize as static inline and removing its header declaration suggests it was needed for local codegen or inlining rather than as an exported function in this build.
Changing Object(NonMatching, "dolphin/ar/arq.c") to Object(Matching, "dolphin/ar/arq.c") is the decisive build-system relink step after source/header/split corrections.
The .data end adjustment for dolphin/dsp/dsp_task.c from 0x80400D80 to 0x80400D7B is a concrete split-map tactic to align section boundaries after relinking.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching src/dolphin/ar/arq.c to see whether static inline helpers remained stable.
Search for ARQGetChunkSize usages to confirm it was not needed as a public header symbol after this relink.
Search other Dolphin SDK relink PRs where functions were changed to static inline and header declarations removed.
Inspect map/split history around dolphin/dsp/dsp_task.c .data boundaries to understand why arq relinking affected that address range.
